tasks.title: Initialize repository structure
acceptance_criteria: Directory tree matches spec exactly: prompts/, docs/, src/, tests/ all exist
src/__init__.py and tests/__init__.py are present (empty or minimal)
README.md exists with project title and one-line description
prompts/architect.md, prompts/engineer.md, prompts/shared_rules.md exist as placeholders
docs/protocol.md and docs/workflow.md exist as placeholders
src/handoff_parser.py, src/task_store.py, src/models.py exist as placeholder stubs
tests/test_handoff_parser.py and tests/test_task_state.py exist as placeholder stubs
No production logic implemented — placeholders only
All files are syntactically valid Python (import-safe stubs) or valid Markdown

tasks.title: Define workflow models and enums
acceptance_criteria: TaskStatus enum defined with values: planned, in_engineering, review, approved, rework, complete
AgentRole enum defined with values: architect, engineer, human
Task dataclass defined with fields: id, title, status, acceptance_criteria, constraints, files_to_touch, changed_files, validation, blockers
HandoffMessage dataclass defined with all handoff block fields plus timestamp
ValidationResult dataclass defined with: valid (bool), errors (list[str])
All models importable from src.models
Models include __repr__ and equality support via dataclass defaults

tasks.title: Implement handoff parser
acceptance_criteria: parse_handoff_file(path) reads a file and returns list[HandoffMessage]
get_latest_valid_block(path) returns the last valid HandoffMessage or None
validate_block(raw_text) returns a ValidationResult with errors for missing required fields
Malformed blocks (missing ---END--- or required fields) are rejected with descriptive errors
Parser is importable from src.handoff_parser

tasks.title: Implement task store
acceptance_criteria: TaskStore class implemented in src/task_store.py
load(path) reads tasks.json and populates internal store
save(path) persists current state to tasks.json
get(task_id) returns Task or raises KeyError
update_status(task_id, new_status) persists status change
list_tasks() returns all tasks
All operations are idempotent for same-status updates

tasks.title: Implement task lifecycle rules
acceptance_criteria: VALID_TRANSITIONS map defined: planned→in_engineering, in_engineering→review, review→approved, review→rework, rework→in_engineering, approved→complete
update_status raises ValueError for invalid transitions with message naming both states
At most one task may be in in_engineering state at a time; attempting a second raises ValueError
Lifecycle rules enforced inside TaskStore.update_status

tasks.title: Add workflow helper logic
acceptance_criteria: derive_next_actor(message: HandoffMessage) -> AgentRole implemented
is_completion_guard_met(tasks: list[Task]) -> bool returns True only when all tasks have status complete
needs_human_escalation(message: HandoffMessage) -> bool returns True when NEXT==human or STATUS==needs_human
Helpers importable from src.workflow or src.handoff_parser
Each helper has a docstring explaining its contract

tasks.title: Write tests
acceptance_criteria: test_handoff_parser.py covers: valid block parse, malformed block rejection, missing required field, multi-block file with mixed valid/invalid, get_latest_valid_block
test_task_state.py covers: all valid transition paths, invalid transition raises ValueError, in_engineering concurrency guard, load/save round-trip
An end-to-end test parses a handoff, updates task store, derives next actor
All tests pass with: python -m pytest tests/
No external test dependencies beyond pytest

tasks.title: Finalize documentation
acceptance_criteria: README.md contains: project title, description, setup instructions, usage examples, file layout
docs/protocol.md documents the full handoff block format with field definitions and allowed values
docs/workflow.md describes the agent workflow with phase descriptions and state transitions
prompts/architect.md contains the architect role prompt usable as a system prompt
prompts/engineer.md contains the engineer role prompt usable as a system prompt
prompts/shared_rules.md contains the session rules shared by both agents

tasks.title: Final review and completion
acceptance_criteria: Architect has reviewed all deliverables against their acceptance criteria
All tests pass
File layout matches spec exactly
handoff.md integrity check: all blocks are well-formed, append-only invariant holds
Architect appends STATUS: plan_complete block to declare completion
